what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that links an image asset to the design-source channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
Fields include id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-source/deck1-2026/p-05
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset
standouts: The target URI 'image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-05' references a specific deck page
conventions: Identifier follows an 'att-' prefix with a hash
Target uses a custom image:// URI scheme
concerns: No title, caption, or description for the image
Target URI cannot be verified from this file alone
notes: A bare attachment record that registers a deck image page under the design-source channel.
questions: What visual content is on deck1-2026 page 05?
Who added this attachment and why?
How is the image:// URI resolved in storage?
purpose_vs_actual: The file acts as an attachment record and does exactly that; it records an image attachment with provenance metadata.
intention_signals: It appears to catalog a specific image page from a design deck into the design-source channel so it can be referenced and traced later.
novelty: The target URI encodes both a deck identifier and a page number, treating a deck page as a first-class image asset.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, typed link between a channel and an external image asset, preserving provenance without embedding the asset itself.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record in the design-source channel pointing to image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-05 with an ISO timestamp and no body content.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to register and timestamp an image asset from a design deck as an attachment to the design-source channel.
